retrieved.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
retrieved.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

retrieved.title: Admission to master programmes
retrieved.text:
Choose a degree programme to receive detailed information regarding the admission procedure. Your steps vary depending on

- if you have completed your bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna or another university, and
- if there are any qualitative admission requirements (QZB) prescribed in the curriculum, and
- if a selection procedure (AV for "Aufnahmeverfahren") is conducted for the master programme of your choice, and
- if the language of instruction of the degree programme is English or German.

Use the selection below. There you will find a step-by-step introduction on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure.

retrieved.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
retrieved.text:
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- if applicable proof of [English language proficiency/knowledge of other languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) according to the curriculum
- further documents specified in the [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/#c124164) (e.g. description of qualifications)

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. To find the overview choose the programme you are interested in from the list at [Admission > Master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

retrieved.text: programmes, you will need subject-specific prior knowledge. You must provide proof of this when you apply for admission. Proof is provided either in the form of a trancript of records from the University of Vienna or a description of your qualifications (= a description of your achievements). The curricula of the master programmes or the Master Access Guide (MAG) indicate which extension curricula (EC) will provide you with the subject-specific prior knowledge. You have the following options: 1. If you have been admitted to a Bachelor's programme at the University of Vienna, you will already complete the required EC during the course of this programme. You do not need a qualification description, your transcript of records is sufficient proof. 2. You did not study at the University of Vienna? Compare the curriculum of your degree programme with the admission requirements listed in the EC curriculum and enter the relevant achievements from your previous degree programme in the "Description of Qualifications" form. Upload the form as proof with your application for admission. - Qualitative admission requirements (QZB)

  Admission to a master programme is only possible if you have completed an eligible bachelor/diploma programme. For some master programmes, you must also fulfil so-called qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen", QZB) when submitting your application. QZB are requirement for admission. You can therefore not make up for these as part of the degree programme you are applying for (e.g. as supplementary examinations).

  QZB are a certain number of ECTS that you must have already completed in parts of your previous degree programme. In addition, individual documents may also be required from you, e.g. a description of qualifications or proof of language proficiency. You can find out whether QZB are required for your master programme either in the [curriculum of the respective degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (in German only) or in the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271).

To be admitted to a master programme, you must have completed a bachelor/diploma programme in the same discipline (= eligible degree programme). In the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/), you can find out with which previous degree programmes and under which conditions admission to a specific master programme is possible. If your previous degree programme is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the specific master programme, a decision whether admission is possible will be made on a case-by-case basis. The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

Master students of other universities can get enrolled to an eligible degree programme at the University of Vienna for one semester as [Visiting Master](/en/admission/master-programmes/visiting-master/) students.

reranked.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
reranked.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

If your previous degree is not listed in the Master Access Guide of the respective degree programme, your application will be checked individually as a decision has to be made on a case-by-case basis. To do this, you must apply for admission to the degree programme by the relevant deadline. The University of Vienna can only assess the eligibility of your previous degree on the basis of your complete application. Preliminary assessments are not possible.

The admission requirements according to university law and the applicable master [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) are binding.

[## You've got further questions?](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

[You get further information at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) of your degree programme.](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/)

reranked.title: Admission to master programmes
reranked.text:
Choose a degree programme to receive detailed information regarding the admission procedure. Your steps vary depending on

- if you have completed your bachelor/diploma programme at the University of Vienna or another university, and
- if there are any qualitative admission requirements (QZB) prescribed in the curriculum, and
- if a selection procedure (AV for "Aufnahmeverfahren") is conducted for the master programme of your choice, and
- if the language of instruction of the degree programme is English or German.

Use the selection below. There you will find a step-by-step introduction on how to submit your application and complete the admission procedure.

reranked.title: I am from ... and want to study at the University of Vienna. What do I have to keep in mind?
heading_path: I want to study a master programme
General requirements for the application for admission to master programmes
reranked.text:
- scan of your valid passport or personal ID (front and back) as JPEG or PNG file
- - higher education degree certificate and transcripts/diploma supplement
    - including the required [legalisation](/en/admission/info-third-country-students/legalisation/) according to the issuing country
    - and a sworn translation (if not issued in German or English)

  - for German and multilingual programmes: [proof of German language proficiency](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) at level A2 or higher
- if applicable proof of [English language proficiency/knowledge of other languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) according to the curriculum
- further documents specified in the [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/#c124164) (e.g. description of qualifications)

You can find information regarding further requirements (e.g. qualitative admission requirements, letter of motivation, language proficiency, ...) on the overview page of the individual degree programme. To find the overview choose the programme you are interested in from the list at [Admission > Master programmes](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/).

You have to submit your application for admission to the degree programme within the application period. The application period depends on your citizenships. [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) have a special application period.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text:
/

- Qualitative admission requirements (QZB)

  Admission to a master programme is only possible if you have completed an eligible bachelor/diploma programme. For some master programmes, you must also fulfil so-called qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen", QZB) when submitting your application. QZB are requirement for admission. You can therefore not make up for these as part of the degree programme you are applying for (e.g. as supplementary examinations).

  QZB are a certain number of ECTS that you must have already completed in parts of your previous degree programme. In addition, individual documents may also be required from you, e.g. a description of qualifications or proof of language proficiency. You can find out whether QZB are required for your master programme either in the [curriculum of the respective degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/) (in German only) or in the [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/#c167271).

selected.title: Master Access Guide (MAG)
selected.text:
Do you want to study a master programme at the University of Vienna? The prerequisite for this is the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme (= in the same discipline) at a post-secondary educational institution.

In the MAG you’ll learn:

- which degree programmes are considered as eligible for your chosen degree programme
- if you need to fulfil any qualitative admission prerequisites for admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.
- if you need to fulfil any supplementary  examinations after admission to your chosen degree programme and, if you do, which.

Question: What are the general prerequisites for master admission, and is there a visiting option?

generate.raw_response: The general prerequisites for master admission at the University of Vienna are the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme in the same discipline [1][5]. A visiting option, called "Visiting Master", is available for one semester, but applicants must meet certain admission requirements, such as presenting a letter of recommendation from their home university [4].

answer: The general prerequisites for master admission at the University of Vienna are the completion of an eligible bachelor or diploma programme in the same discipline [1][5]. A visiting option, called "Visiting Master", is available for one semester, but applicants must meet certain admission requirements, such as presenting a letter of recommendation from their home university [4].
